behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because correctness depends on real speech-model diarization output (speaker labeling quality), which a stub would mask (LLM-DEP/MOCK-HIDE). I considered Workflow, but the behavior text focuses on one model output property ("speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"), not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments include correct speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text and can fail within one function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor holds). I considered Integration, but behavior text is local ("Transcript parsing extracts...") and does not require cross-module handoff evidence.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments and verify produced structure includes correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because the pass condition depends on what the real summarizer model chooses to include for each assigned attendee; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide omission failures (LLM-DEP). I considered Workflow, but the behavior text targets one live-summary quality criterion, not a multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript (or structured summary input) with multiple explicit attendee assignments, invoke the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified; this is local deterministic transformation and does not inherently require external I/O or model judgment. I considered Integration, but the text "routes identified decisions" disqualifies higher-level dependency as the core failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call the routing function with pre-identified decision objects and assert output schema places them under `decisions` and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output quality/placement in generated summary sections, so real LLM behavior is required (LLM-DEP; deterministic-assertion trap applies). I considered Workflow, but behavior text asks for one output property in the live summary, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing clear decisions and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow because behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing over segments plus merged final coherence; this is multiple agentic calls in sequence (MULTI-STEP). Integration is too low due LLM/STT content dependence, and System alone is too low because correctness is not just infra execution but cross-step output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording, execute production segmentation + sequential transcription/summarization path, and verify segment order, complete merge coverage, and coherence/consistency of the final merged summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans end-to-end pipeline stages with real recording and no manual intervention; it requires real infrastructure plus multiple agentic steps (transcription + summarization) in sequence. Lower levels are disqualified by behavior text "full pipeline" and "real meeting recording."
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end test in a production-like environment: upload a real meeting file, execute transcription and summarization through real services, and assert successful structured output delivery without operator actions.
